system_description.overview: Clicks Inc. provides an AI agent platform that automates repetitive back-office desktop tasks using computer use (clicking and typing). Customers delegate tasks via a dedicated email address and receive results in the same thread; each AI agent gets its own virtual computer mirroring human interaction with existing customer systems.

products.Task delegation via email: Customers send tasks to a custom Clicks email address; a 24/7-monitored AI agent completes the work and replies in-thread.
products.AI computer use agents: Each customer receives customized AI agents with access to their specific tools (CRM, LinkedIn, etc.); an arbitrary number can run in parallel and proactively flag issues.
products.Guacamole Server: Apache Guacamole is used as the remote-desktop gateway component for agent VM access, visible in the network diagram.

infrastructure.physical_presence: None — no physical offices or physical servers; fully cloud-hosted
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (report states 'spinning up multiple servers across all availability zones in Azure')

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Azure VNET with two subnets: 'Subnet agents' (Agent VMs) and 'Subnet guacamole' (Guacamole Server). Traffic from the internet flows through a NAT gateway into the VNET.
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: Azure IDS/IPS employed via Azure security policy network settings.
network_architecture.vpn: Bastion host with VPN for remote access.
network_architecture.tls: TLS (Transport Layer Security) used for all user requests; certificates from established third-party CA.
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; endpoints restricted to internet via this filter.
network_architecture.topology: Internet → NAT Gateway → Azure VNET (Norway East) → Subnet agents (Agent VMs) + Subnet guacamole (Guacamole Server). Remote admin access via bastion host/VPN.

application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments; changes tested before deployment to Stage or Production.

data_storage.backup_frequency: Formal backup policy exists; frequency not specified beyond 'suitable backups taken and maintained'

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Azure Active Directory (passwords controlled through Azure Password Policy)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform; also required to access source code management tool.
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Azure AD password policy: periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements, account lockout after limited failed attempts. Guest and anonymous logins not allowed. Unattended desktops lock on inactivity.

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest (Azure KMS implied)
encryption.key_management: Azure cloud provider key management service; access keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Source code management tool (specific vendor not named); access restricted via MFA
ci_cd_devops.ci_cd_tool: Not named explicitly; change management tool referenced for tracking changes initiation through deployment
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured so every merge request to production requires additional approval (CC8.1.2)
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline implementation and rollback; 'baseline configurations retained within infrastructure as a code'
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO; monthly patch management reviews; workstations scanned daily for patch compliance; OS patches for network devices auto-updated

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Internal monitoring tool (vendor not named); monitors system capacity and performance, generates alerts on predefined thresholds
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Azure-based logging; logs cover admin activities, logon attempts, data deletions, function changes, security configurations, permissions/roles
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts to IT management for high and critical risk events; resolved via incident management process

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure, data center, IDS/IPS, key management, AD/passwords
Remote desktop gateway for agent VM access

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and malware protection on all production servers (continuous auto-scan; weekly definition updates) and Mac workstations/laptops.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; tool not named. Self-assessment scan before every production release or annually.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party.

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical access to facilities: Azure is responsible; Clicks has no physical office
A1.2 — Environmental protections: managed by building management / Azure
Processing Integrity trust criteria — determined not applicable
Privacy trust criteria — determined not applicable
notable_controls: CC8.1.3: Source code access restricted to MFA-authenticated authorized users
CC8.1.2: Branch protection — every merge to production requires additional approval
CC6.2.2: Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged
CC6.1.5: Cloud provider KMS for encryption key management
CC7.1.1: IaC baseline configurations with rollback capability

infrastructure.rationale: Azure Norway East, multi-AZ failover, VPC with segmented subnets, IDS/IPS, bastion/VPN. Single region only; no CDN; no IaC tooling named explicitly.

application_architecture.rationale: Agent VM + Guacamole architecture is novel but minimal tech disclosure. No languages, frameworks, or API style named. Separate environments confirmed, branch protection confirmed.

data_layer.rationale: No specific database, caching, or storage technologies named. Backup policy exists but frequency undisclosed. Per-tenant segregation confirmed. Significant opacity.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen test, monthly vuln scans, IDS/IPS via Azure, firewalls, MFA on admin access, antivirus on all endpoints, KMS encryption at rest. No WAF named, no SIEM named.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC confirmed (unnamed tool), branch protection with required approvals, separate environments, emergency change process, monthly patch reviews. CI/CD tool not named.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and annually tested, multi-AZ failover via Azure. No RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region failover, daily backup frequency not confirmed.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Extremely Azure-dependent — only two third-party vendors identified (Azure + Guacamole). Single critical cloud dependency with no named secondary providers.

overall.rationale: Clicks has a reasonable security control framework for an early-stage AI automation company. Core controls (MFA, IDS, pen testing, quarterly access reviews, BCDR) are present. Key weaknesses: single-region Azure, very limited tech stack disclosure, no named CI/CD or monitoring tools, extreme Azure concentration risk.

red_flags: Extreme single-vendor concentration: essentially all infrastructure, IDS/IPS, identity, KMS, and availability relies on Microsoft Azure alone.
No CI/CD tool named — deployment automation maturity cannot be assessed.
No specific database or storage technologies disclosed — data layer opacity.
No WAF mentioned despite web-facing product.
Single region (Norway East) — no geographic redundancy.
yellow_flags: Backup frequency not specified beyond 'suitable backups taken and maintained'.
SIEM not named — log aggregation and correlation capability unclear.
Source code management tool vendor not named.
green_flags: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party.
Monthly automated vulnerability scans with remediation tracking.
Multi-AZ failover architecture with AZ-level redundancy.
MFA enforced for admin access and source code tool.
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts.
Access revocation within one business day of termination.
Branch protection with required PR approvals for production merges.
IaC with rollback capability for infrastructure changes.
Annual BCDR testing across defined loss scenarios.
Guacamole-based agent VM architecture is an interesting security boundary — agents isolated in dedicated subnets.
key_observations: Clicks uses Apache Guacamole as a remote desktop gateway — this is a non-trivial architectural choice that provides browser-based access to agent VMs without requiring direct VM access, creating a useful security boundary.
The agent VM model (each AI agent gets its own VM) means the attack surface scales with customer count — isolation between agent VMs and the Guacamole server subnet is important.

Norway East as the sole region is unusual for a US-incorporated Delaware company (goclicks.ai) — likely chosen for cost/availability reasons but creates latency risk for US customers and regulatory questions.
No Availability (A1) criteria were in scope — surprising for a product where uptime is critical for automated workflows. The company excluded A1.2 due to relying on Azure.
Processing Integrity and Privacy criteria were explicitly excluded — relevant if customers process sensitive data through AI agents.
The CC8.1.3 control confirms source code access requires MFA, but the source code tool vendor is unnamed — could be GitHub, GitLab, or other.
